items.headline: Philippines declares national energy emergency as fuel reserves drop below two months
items.body: Manila became the first nation to formally declare energy emergency status, citing critically low oil supplies. With the country importing nearly all petroleum from the Middle East, reserves have fallen to around 45 days.

items.headline: First UN-backed troops deploy to Haiti as gang violence kills dozens in central regions
items.body: Chadian advance forces arrived alongside mission commander to launch expanded Gang Suppression Force. The deployment follows massacre of over 70 civilians in Artibonite as armed groups expand control beyond Port-au-Prince.

items.headline: Japan activates long-range missiles in Kyushu, placing Chinese mainland within strike range
items.body: Tokyo deployed domestically-developed Type 25 systems capable of hitting targets beyond 1,000 kilometers. The move marks Japan's first operational counterstrike capability amid rising tensions in the East China Sea.

items.headline: Burkina Faso forces killed twice as many civilians as militants since 2023, rights group finds
items.body: Government troops and allied militias killed over 1,800 civilians in ethnic cleansing campaign, Human Rights Watch reported. The atrocities amount to crimes against humanity receiving minimal global attention.

no.headline: Electric vehicles hit 98.4% of new car sales as Norway completes fossil fuel phase-out
no.body: <cite index="21-2,21-3">Norway registered 17,406 battery-electric vehicles in March, accounting for 98.4 percent of all new passenger cars—a monthly record.</cite> <cite index="21-8">Only 279 vehicles with alternative drivetrains were registered, including 22 petrol and 126 diesel cars.</cite>

nl.headline: Four major Dutch cities urge Senate to reject criminalizing illegal residence ahead of April 14 vote
nl.body: Amsterdam, Utrecht, Groningen, and Eindhoven publicly challenged the Senate to vote against making undocumented residence a criminal offense, warning the measure is unenforceable and would drive vulnerable residents away from essential services. The municipalities argue children would lose access to education and healthcare as undocumented parents avoid all government contact to escape prosecution.
